# autoFill / --fill only generates the message when creating a change
# request. Regenerating the title and body of an existing change
# request requires the explicit --regenerate-message flag.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-04-05T16:40:32Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# create and submit a branch with --fill using the first generator
chmod 755 $WORK/gen1.sh
git config spice.message.generator $WORK/gen1.sh

git add feature.txt
gs bc -m 'Add feature' feature1
gs branch submit --fill
stderr 'Created #'

shamhub dump changes
stdout 'First Title'

# swap in a generator that would produce different output
chmod 755 $WORK/gen2.sh
git config spice.message.generator $WORK/gen2.sh

# re-submit with --fill after a change: the branch is pushed, but the
# title and body are left untouched (the generator does not run).
cp $WORK/extra/feature-update.txt feature.txt
git add feature.txt
git commit -m 'update feature'
gs branch submit --fill
stderr 'Updated #'

shamhub dump changes
stdout 'First Title'
! stdout 'Second Title'

# re-submit with --regenerate-message: the generator runs and
# overwrites the existing title and body.
gs branch submit --regenerate-message
stderr 'Updated #'

shamhub dump changes
stdout 'Second Title'
! stdout 'First Title'

-- repo/feature.txt --
Contents of feature
-- extra/feature-update.txt --
New contents of feature
-- gen1.sh --
#!/bin/sh
printf '{"title":"First Title","body":"first body"}'
-- gen2.sh --
#!/bin/sh
printf '{"title":"Second Title","body":"second body"}'
